_comment: Per-domain collection strategy. 29 of 35 hypotheses share the 'ohlcv_backbone' plan; the 6 domain-specific hypotheses each get their own plan. See data_feasibility_report.md for the full reasoning behind each choice.

ohlcv_backbone.merge_logic: fetch_with_failover() walks the chain in order, first successful provider wins for that request; no cross-provider merging within one fetch. _deepen_with_history() separately left-extends a short live-fetched frame with the on-disk deep-history archive when the live fetch alone doesn't cover the requested lookback.
ohlcv_backbone.conflict_resolution: no automatic conflict resolution between providers exists today — core/data_confidence.py cross-checks independent providers on carrier symbols and flags disagreement for human review rather than silently picking one.
ohlcv_backbone.timestamp_normalization: all providers normalized to UTC at the loader boundary (core/data_loader.py, core/data_providers.py).
ohlcv_backbone.timezone_normalization: UTC throughout; no local-timezone data enters the pipeline.
ohlcv_backbone.quality_checks: core/data_validator.validate_ohlcv() (nulls, duplicate timestamps, malformed OHLC relationships, monotonic index); main.py DATA STARVATION logging when NNFX's >=210-bar or the D1 MTF gate's >=50-bar floor is violated.
ohlcv_backbone.known_gap_not_closed: FCS API's 300-candle/request cap is not paginated around — acceptable because FCS is fallback-only in every chain it appears in, never primary.

H007_macro.merge_logic: load_macro_snapshot() (core/alt_data_loader.py) tries CBOE first for VIX, falls to FRED VIXCLS on failure; all other series go directly to FRED, no fallback needed (single official source per series).
H007_macro.conflict_resolution: not applicable — one authoritative series per macro variable, no competing providers.
H007_macro.duplicate_removal: handled by the same OHLCV-contract loader path (core/alt_data_loader.py: FRED/CBOE-style close series -> OHLCV contract).
H007_macro.quality_checks: none beyond the shared loader validation today; recommended addition: flag any FRED series revision (rare, government data revises on defined schedules) rather than silently overwriting a prior value.
H007_macro.known_gap_not_closed: none — this domain is fully served by free official sources.

H012_cot.primary_backfill_recommended_not_yet_bui: cftc_cot_yearly_archive — new script needed, e.g. scripts/download_cot_deep_history.py, reusing scripts/download_cot.py's existing column-layout parser against CFTC's yearly deacot archive files instead of the current-week file
H012_cot.fallback: none — CFTC is the sole authoritative source for this data by definition
H012_cot.merge_logic: yearly-archive backfill would populate data/cot/{SYMBOL}.json's history array up to the point where the weekly rolling collector's own accumulated history takes over — no overlap-resolution needed if the backfill script stops exactly at the weekly collector's earliest cached week.
H012_cot.timestamp_normalization: COT reports are published Fridays ~15:30 ET for the prior Tuesday's positioning; existing engine consumes report-date, not publish-date — must preserve this convention in any new backfill script to avoid a look-ahead mismatch against the live collector's semantics.
H012_cot.timezone_normalization: convert ET report dates to UTC consistently with the existing collector.
H012_cot.quality_checks_needed: contract-name changes across years (CFTC occasionally renames/reclassifies instruments), missing weeks around government shutdowns (a real historical gap, not a bug), duplicate week detection.
H012_cot.known_gap_not_closed: the backfill script itself does not exist yet — this is the plan's one concrete action item outside the OHLCV backbone.

H019_crypto_positioning.status: RESOLVED — hypothesis already FAILED; plan retained for the evidence chain, not for further action

dropped.open_interest: dropped per H019's own pre-registered fallback rule — none of binance/bybit/okx reached institutional depth

H019_crypto_positioning.merge_logic: no merge needed — funding rate and Fear&Greed are independent series, combined only at the modulator's scoring step (confluence/crypto_positioning_modulator.py), never at the data layer.
H019_crypto_positioning.timestamp_normalization: funding rate keyed to settlement timestamp (00:00/08:00/16:00 UTC fixed schedule); Fear&Greed keyed to publish timestamp (daily).
H019_crypto_positioning.quality_checks: causal_context_at() enforces strictly-before alignment for both series — the look-ahead guard this hypothesis's own pre-registration required before any code ran.
H019_crypto_positioning.known_gap_not_closed: none actionable — the hypothesis is resolved; OI would need a new hypothesis to revisit, not a plan update here.

H021_marketaux_sentiment.fallback: none adopted — NewsAPI.org and GDELT were evaluated as candidates (data_sources_registry.json) but a fallback swap mid-hypothesis would violate CLAUDE.md rule 6 (never change entries/data mid-sample); either could be a NEW pre-registered hypothesis if MarketAux's accumulated sample proves too thin
H021_marketaux_sentiment.merge_logic: not applicable — single source in use.
H021_marketaux_sentiment.timestamp_normalization: published_at normalized to UTC at collection time.
H021_marketaux_sentiment.duplicate_removal: needed at the article level (same story often re-syndicated) — verify scripts/collect_marketaux_sentiment.py dedupes on (symbol, published_at, source) before H021's actual evaluation run.
H021_marketaux_sentiment.quality_checks: XAUUSD->GOLD entity-mapping correctness already caught and fixed 2026-07-24; verify no other symbol silently maps to a wrong/empty entity before evaluation.
H021_marketaux_sentiment.known_gap_not_closed: iatis-marketaux-collect.timer enablement status on the VPS was not confirmed as of the last registry note — verify it is actually running before assuming data is accumulating.

H104_binance_orderflow.primary: binance_aggtrade_ws (scripts/collect_binance_orderflow.py + iatis-orderflow-collector.service, confirmed connected and running 2026-07-24)
H104_binance_orderflow.fallback_commercial_optional: tardis_dev — could eliminate the 3-month forward-only wait via historical tick backfill; price unverified, treat as its own explicit purchase decision if the wait is judged unacceptable, not a default action
H104_binance_orderflow.merge_logic: not applicable — single forward-collecting source; a future Tardis.dev backfill (if purchased) would need explicit merge/dedup logic against the live-collected bars at the seam date, not yet designed since no purchase has been made.
H104_binance_orderflow.timestamp_normalization: trade_ts_ms floored to 15-minute bar boundaries via bar_start_ms(), UTC.
H104_binance_orderflow.duplicate_removal: not applicable — single append-only stream, one bar emitted per completed interval per symbol.
H104_binance_orderflow.quality_checks: parse_agg_trade() returns None (never raises) on malformed messages; reconnect-with-backoff on any drop; DeltaAccumulator.is_empty() suppresses zero-trade bars from being written.
H104_binance_orderflow.known_gap_not_closed: no historical backfill exists or can exist for the pre-collection period — this is an accepted, pre-registered constraint of the hypothesis itself, not a gap to close.

H105_execution_quality.primary: storage/execution_quality.py (first-party TCA ledger, live since 2026-07-16, Cloudflare D1-backed)
H105_execution_quality.fallback: none — no external vendor could substitute for this project's own real fills
H105_execution_quality.timestamp_normalization: already UTC, tagged with active session (regimes/session_context.py) at write time.
H105_execution_quality.duplicate_removal: not applicable — one row per real broker fill.
H105_execution_quality.quality_checks: dry-run fills excluded by design (would dilute the statistic); sign-convention documented and unit-consistent with the backtest engine's pip convention.
H105_execution_quality.known_gap_not_closed: none — purely time-gated on live trading volume accumulating past the pre-registered n>=100 floor.
